author: wolovim
content:
⚡ friendly reminder that there are two H* headliner breakout calls this week:

1️⃣ LUCID Encrypted Mempool - weds @ 15:00 UTC
- occurring during the recurring Encrypt the Mempool breakout call
- https://github.com/ethereum/pm/issues/1945
- cc/ <@827653956955406406> 

2️⃣ EIP-8141 Frame Transaction (announced directly above) - thurs @ 15:30 UTC
- https://github.com/ethereum/pm/issues/1954
- cc/ <@543900561460822016> <@194432762315407360> 

the goal is to make a confident headliner decision by next ACDE call, so add to each agenda by commenting on the github issue above with what you want to discuss or see addressed. 

*note that these are not the only headliners up for consideration, but a breakout to further understand these two proposals was requested in the last ACDE call. full list: https://forkcast.org/upgrade/hegota#headliner-proposals

author: ralexstokes
content:
Hey everyone!

Myself and others have been reflecting on call cadence given that recent ACDC calls have been on the short side and in part prompted by similar observations around “too many calls” in [this thread](https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/1478010793042903101).

I do think in general we should be responsive to what we currently need and given where we are currently at with epbs-devnets (waiting for launch of devent-0 before we can do _that_ much more) and a relatively light agenda for this week’s call I’m going to **cancel the synchronous call this week on 5 March**.

Instead, I think it’s a good opportunity to experiment with async work (where it is sufficient), so I’ll make a thread here where we can handle the agenda items. The idea is not to “skip” the week’s agenda, but to see how much we can do asynchronously. **Please check the thread for some discussion points to address this week.** I do expect to need a synchronous call on 19 March so please keep that on your calendars. If it turns out we do need synchronous discussions before 19 March, I’ll find some during next week’s ACDT or ACDE.

(happy epbs-devnet-0 🙂 )
